data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract â€” every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose â€” do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260507T131757-5b960d10/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing â€” do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** â€” fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260507T131757-5b960d10/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale â‡’ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260507T131757-5b960d10/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` â€” the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` â€” bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260507T131757-5b960d10/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** â€” out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260507T131757-5b960d10/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree â€” write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch â€” meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact â€” DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** â€” the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator â€” do not keep exploring or testing.

## Decomposition depth cap
This bead is already at decomposition depth 2. Do not create another child layer; if it is still too large, reject the split with a short explanation and write no_changes_rationale.txt instead.

  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-36eb046c">
    <title>work: serialize model-backed readiness before claim races</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
`ddx work` now runs model-backed bead readiness intake and bead-lifecycle lint after selecting a ready bead but before atomically claiming it. With two workers on the same project, both workers can select the same bead, both spend model calls on intake/lint, and both can append pre-dispatch events before one loses `Store.Claim`. If intake returns `actionable_but_rewritten`, the loser can also mutate bead description/acceptance before owning the bead. Atomic `Store.Claim` still prevents duplicate implementation, but it no longer protects the expensive and stateful pre-claim phase.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:686-765 calls `runtime.PreClaimIntakeHook` and handles rewrite/block outcomes before `w.Store.Claim` is attempted.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:768-807 calls `runtime.PreDispatchLintHook` and `appendPreDispatchLintEvent` before the claim, so concurrent workers can append duplicate lint events for the same open bead.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:809-823 only detects the ownership race after intake/lint have already run.
- cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_rewrite.go:39-58 loads and updates bead description/acceptance from the intake rewrite path without a prior worker claim/ownership check.
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:588-635 makes `ClaimWithOptions` the atomic ownership boundary, but `ReadyExecution` at cli/internal/bead/store.go:1352-1355 only lists candidates and does not reserve them.

PROPOSED FIX
- Move the expensive/model-backed readiness and lint gates behind an atomic worker reservation, or add an equivalent atomic `ReserveForReadiness` lease that removes the bead from other workers while intake/lint runs.
- If the chosen design uses ordinary claim before readiness: claim first, start heartbeat, run intake and lint, then unclaim/release on retryable intake errors, park with `needs_human`/triage metadata on terminal readiness outcomes, and continue to implementation only for actionable beads.
- If the chosen design uses a separate readiness lease: make `ReadyExecution` exclude fresh readiness leases, make stale leases reclaimable, and require rewrite/lint event mutations to verify lease ownership.
- Ensure `actionable_but_rewritten` applies metadata under the same ownership/lease used by the worker, so concurrent workers cannot double-rewrite or append conflicting events.
- Preserve the existing atomic `Store.Claim` duplicate-implementation guarantee and existing `picker.claim_race` telemetry for true claim races.

NON-SCOPE
- Changing the bead-lifecycle rubric or model prompt content.
- Removing readiness/lint checks entirely.
- Changing final implementation landing/review semantics.

DEPS
No deps. This is a queue safety bug introduced by the current pre-claim intake ordering and can be fixed independently of the parked-bead triage work.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. `TestExecuteBeadWorkerConcurrentPreClaimIntakeRunsOncePerBead` starts two workers against one ready bead with a blocking `PreClaimIntakeHook` and proves only one worker enters the model-backed intake for that bead while the other observes it as unavailable or moves on.
2. `TestExecuteBeadWorkerConcurrentPreDispatchLintDoesNotDuplicateEvents` proves two workers cannot append duplicate `bead-quality.lint` events for the same readiness attempt before ownership is established.
3. `TestPreClaimIntakeRewriteRequiresOwnedReservation` proves `actionable_but_rewritten` cannot update description/acceptance unless the worker owns the claim/readiness lease, and concurrent losers leave the bead unchanged.
4. `TestExecuteBeadWorkerReadinessRejectReleasesOrParksClaim` covers intake error, ambiguous/needs-human, and lint-block outcomes: retryable errors release the bead or set a bounded cooldown; terminal readiness outcomes park with explicit labels/metadata; actionable beads proceed to implementation.
5. Existing `TestExecuteBeadWorkerConcurrentWorkersDoNotDoubleExecuteSameBead` and `TestExecuteLoop_TwoWorkersBothClaimP0sBeforeP2s` still pass, preserving duplicate-implementation protection and priority ordering.
6. WIRED-IN: production `ExecuteBeadWorker.Run` uses the new claim/reservation boundary before invoking `PreClaimIntakeHook`, `applyPreClaimIntakeRewrite`, or `appendPreDispatchLintEvent`.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... -run "TestExecuteBeadWorkerConcurrentPreClaim|TestExecuteBeadWorkerConcurrentPreDispatch|TestPreClaimIntakeRewriteRequiresOwnedReservation|TestExecuteBeadWorkerReadinessReject|TestExecuteBeadWorkerConcurrentWorkersDoNotDoubleExecuteSameBead|TestExecuteLoop_TwoWorkersBothClaimP0sBeforeP2s" -count=1 passes.
8. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... passes.
9. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:2, area:agent, area:beads, kind:bug, reliability, bead-quality, concurrency, spec:FEAT-010, adr:023</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-a9d130d0" spec-id="FEAT-010" base-rev="7c681820bd48192f64f90dd7bb42aab07e85f527" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260507T131757-5b960d10"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="FEAT-010" path="docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md">Feature: Task Execution</ref>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>
